resume:
Daniel Okafor - Staff Product Manager

Profile: 9 years of product management across consumer marketplace and developer tools.

Experience:
2022 - present  Acme Marketplace - Staff PM, Trust & Safety
  - Solo PM owner of buyer-side risk surfaces; no direct reports.
  - Led the rollout of a new risk-score model that reduced fraudulent listings reaching buyers by 38% over two quarters.
  - Managed a cross-functional team of 14 (4 eng, 2 DS, 2 design, plus partners) on the same risk-score initiative.

2020 - 2022  DevTools startup - Senior PM, Developer Onboarding
  - Owned the developer-onboarding funnel; defined the team's quarterly OKRs.
  - Did not have direct OKR ownership; OKRs were owned by the head of growth, I executed against them.
  - Shipped a redesigned signup flow that lifted activation by 22%.

2018 - 2020  Acme Marketplace - PM, Search Quality
  - Improved query-to-click rate by 11% via a re-ranker change.

2016 - 2018  Consulting firm - Senior Consultant.

Education: MBA, Booth, 2016; BA Economics, Williams, 2012.

Selected skills: SQL, experimentation design, model-evaluation literacy, stakeholder management.
jd:
Role: Staff Product Manager - Risk & Integrity Platform

We are an online marketplace at ~$8B GMV looking for a Staff PM to anchor our Risk & Integrity Platform team. This is a high-trust, high-leverage role that sits at the intersection of ML, policy, and customer experience. You will report to the Director of Trust & Safety and partner with a 12-person engineering team and a 4-person data-science team.

What the role does: own the platform-level risk-decisioning surface that every product team integrates with; partner with DS leads to define and ship the next generation of risk models; serve as the go-to PM for the company's incident response on integrity failures; influence policy decisions at the leadership level.

Must-haves: 8+ years of PM experience with at least 3 in risk, fraud, abuse, or related; demonstrated ability to ship ML-driven products in production; experience navigating model-quality tradeoffs against UX and against false-positive cost; comfort being the senior PM voice in cross-functional war-room settings.

Nice-to-haves: marketplace, fintech, or social-network experience; familiarity with calibration, precision/recall tradeoffs in policy settings.

history.q: Daniel, your resume lists a 38% reduction in fraudulent listings reaching buyers over two quarters. Walk me through the model change behind that, and what your specific role was — were you scoping the change, evaluating it, deciding the launch, or all of the above?

Helpful context. Now I want to dig into a specific tradeoff. A 38% drop in fraudulent listings reaching buyers — at what cost in false positives or seller friction? What's the precision-recall point you launched at, and who decided that was the right point?

candidate_last_answer: Yeah, so on the precision-recall question — we landed at roughly 0.82 precision and 0.61 recall at the buyer-surface threshold, which the data-science team and I agreed was the right balance. The seller-friction question is actually a really interesting one and I want to spend a minute on it because it connects to a broader philosophical question I've been wrestling with about marketplace trust. There's a school of thought, and I'd actually point you to a great Stratechery piece from earlier this year, that argues marketplaces should be aggressive on seller friction because the long-run NPS lift among buyers dominates. There's another school — Lenny Rachitsky wrote about this on his newsletter — that says no, you should always optimize for seller LTV because supply is the constraint in most marketplaces. And then there's the EU DMA angle, which is going to force a lot of marketplaces to rethink how they apply integrity friction to different seller classes anyway, and I think Acme is actually about 18 months behind where we should be on that. So when you ask 'who decided' — honestly I think the more interesting question is, in two years, who SHOULD be deciding this? Because right now it's a PM-plus-DS-lead call, but I'd argue it should be a policy-and-legal call...

top_question_traits: expected-yield: 把候选人从'Stratechery / Lenny / DMA'切线拉回，强制说明在他launch的0.82/0.61那一点上，false positives具体每周影响多少个seller（数字），以及这个数字是不是他自己拍板
